raw_consumer_statement: Between XXXX and XX/XX/year>, fraudulent activity occurred on my XXXX XXXX XXXX Citibank credit card ( account ending in XXXX ). A total of approximately {$18000.00} was deducted at once from my linked checking account through a scheduled payment. Upon review, I discovered that approximately {$14000.00} of these charges were unauthorized. Critically, the fraudulent transactions never appeared in XXXX XXXX customer app, which is the primary tool provided to customers to monitor transactions. Therefore, I had no way of detecting the fraudulent charges. Only one physical card was issued to me, and I regularly monitored the app to track legitimate purchases. After realizing the discrepancy, I immediately contacted XXXX XXXX by phone. I spent several hours with a representative who carefully reviewed the transactions and confirmed that the disputed charges were not visible in the Home Depot app and thus could not have been identified or authorized by me. On XX/XX/year>, Citibank sent a letter denying my fraud claim, stating that the cards chip was used and therefore they deemed the charges not fraudulent. However, Citibank refused to provide any additional evidence, such as merchant names, receipts, transaction locations, or authentication methods. No appeal process was offered in their letter. I attempted to submit a written dispute through Home Depots secure messaging portal, but the system was broken and repeatedly failed to deliver my messages, leaving me with no functioning method to submit a written dispute as required under Regulation E and the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). Citibanks representatives refused to escalate the matter or provide any investigation documents. The denial is invalid because chip use alone does not prove that a transaction was authorized, especially when card cloning, theft, or merchant fraud could be involved. Citibank violated Regulation E and FCBA by failing to properly investigate, failing to provide an appeal process, failing to supply evidence supporting their denial, and maintaining a broken secure messaging system that blocked my ability to file a dispute in writing. I request that Citibank immediately reverse and refund all fraudulent charges and associated fees, provide full investigation documentation including merchant details and receipts, confirm that no negative credit reporting will occur, and fix the secure messaging system so that customers can submit disputes in writing as required by law. I am attaching the denial letter and can provide additional supporting evidence upon request.
